title: Fix constructor parameters in inheritance hierarchies (#6036)
body:
See #6036 . It has all boiled down to Verilator using virtual inheritance for _everything_ - it should rather keep non-virtual inheritance for the "extends" cases and virtual only for "implements" cases. This gives us two different inheritance hierarchies, one for the linear single-ancestor "extends" chain and a possibly-diamond one for interface classes (which doesn't need construction on the SV side).

The trouble is that with C++ virtual class inheritance, the top level class has to initialize all the virtual ancestor s- and with current SV - C++ mapping, we simply don't have reasonable context / scope in the top class's constructor to properly call the constructor code of the virtual ancestors (the mapped super.new() calls).
